scenario: Aurora Bank wants to group 6 invoices currently held in a FEEL variable by their person attribute in Camunda 8.8 so that downstream rules can produce one sub-list per payer and total invoice amounts per person without writing custom worker code.
question: Which FEEL expression groups the invoices list by the person attribute into a list of sub-lists?

options.text: for p in distinct values(invoices.person) return invoices[person = p] iterates the unique person values and filters each subset
for p in invoices.person return invoices[person = p] iterates every person occurrence and filters the matching invoices each pass
some p in invoices.person satisfies invoices[person = p] tests whether any person value yields a matching invoice sub-list
every p in distinct values(invoices.person) satisfies invoices[person = p] tests whether every person yields a sub-list

a.text: Correct. The documented grouping pattern uses 'for p in distinct values(invoices.person) return invoices[person = p]' to iterate unique persons and filter the invoices for each, yielding a list of sub-lists.

b.text: Incorrect. Iterating invoices.person directly walks every entry and produces duplicate sub-lists; the docs use distinct values to iterate unique persons exactly once.

c.text: Incorrect. The 'some' quantifier returns a boolean indicating that at least one element satisfies the condition; it does not materialize a list of grouped sub-lists.

d.text: Incorrect. The 'every' quantifier also returns a boolean covering all elements; it is not the documented mechanism to produce grouped sub-lists from a list.

explanation: The FEEL grouping example computes distinct person values and, for each, filters the original list with invoices[person = p], yielding a list of sub-lists where each contains the invoices for one person.
